title: Visualizing the Diameter of a Set in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing a set of points in a plane, the distances between them, and highlighting the pair of points that define the set's diameter.
elements: A bounded set S depicted as a cloud of points
Points A and B, the most distant pair in S
The distance d(A, B) labeled as the diameter
A dashed circle illustrating the concept of a bounding sphere
relationships: Points A and B are contained within the set S
The distance between A and B is the supremum of all pairwise distances in S
The diameter δ(S) equals d(A, B)
All other points in S lie within a circle of radius δ(S)/2 centered at the midpoint of A and B
